metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (with a grep or schema read) that `idea` is an established local status recognized by the reducer, the common schema's `ticket_status` enum, and the `transition` CLI тАФ or document in the plan that adding `idea` to those subsystems is also in scope (it is currently absent from all three).
findings.finding: The plan's foundational premise тАФ that 'idea' is an established local status in the rebar codebase тАФ is unverified. The codebase shows 'idea' is entirely absent from every local status vocabulary: common.schema.json ticket_status enum lists ['open','in_progress','blocked','closed','archived','deleted']; inbound_translate._LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES lists ('open','in_progress','blocked','closed','cancelled','done'); transition.py _VALID_STATUSES = ('open','in_progress','closed','blocked'). No search hit for 'idea' as a local status anywhere in src/rebar. The plan treats adding reconciler maps as sufficient, but if 'idea' is not recognized by the reducer, the schema, and the transition machinery, map entries alone cannot make it sync correctly.
findings.location: Plan section 'Why' / 'What' тАФ the premise that 'For idea to sync, the reconciler must translate it'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the plan scope to include: (a) adding 'idea' to common.schema.json ticket_status enum, (b) adding 'idea' to _LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES in inbound_translate.py (not just as a comment note), (c) adding 'idea' to _VALID_STATUSES in transition.py if localтЖТidea transitions are intended. If 'idea' is intentionally a Jira-only status that is never set locally via the CLI (i.e. the ticket arrives inbound and stays as-is), document that constraint explicitly and note that the transition CLI will reject any attempt to set a local ticket to 'idea'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (`ticket_status` enum) and the generated `src/rebar/types.py` (`TicketStatus` Literal) need to include `"idea"` тАФ if so, add them to the Scope and What sections (and regenerate `types.py` via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` per the drift-gate).
findings.finding: The plan adds `idea` to all reconciler status maps but never enumerates `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (the `ticket_status` enum) or the generated `src/rebar/types.py` (`TicketStatus = Literal[...]`) as edit targets. Both currently exclude `idea` from the recognized status vocabulary. Schema validators, typed APIs, and the gen_types drift-gate all consume this enum тАФ if `idea` is not added there, any code path that validates a ticket's status field against the schema (or that type-checks against `TicketStatus`) will reject `idea`-status tickets inbound-created or translated by the reconciler.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## What тАФ no mention of common.schema.json or src/rebar/types.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` and `src/rebar/types.py` to the Scope section. In What, add: 'Add `"idea"` to the `ticket_status` enum in `common.schema.json` and regenerate `src/rebar/types.py` via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`.' Add a corresponding AC: '`TicketStatus` includes `"idea"` and `make lint` (drift-gate check) passes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test files/functions to Scope and What for: (a) an outbound-fields unit test asserting `idea тЖТ 'IDEA'`, (b) an inbound-differ/translate unit test asserting `'IDEA' тЖТ idea`, and (c) a preflight unit test confirming no abort on a local `idea` status тАФ so ACs 7, 8, and 9 each have a concrete proving step and a named test location.
findings.finding: ACs 7, 8, and 9 claim test-verified outcomes (outbound translation, inbound translation, preflight non-abort) but no corresponding test files or test steps are listed in the What section or Scope тАФ only `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py` (the parity test) appears in scope, leaving three ACs with no described proving step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria lines 7тАУ9; Scope section; What section
findings.suggested_fix:
Add three scope entries:
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_outbound_fields.py` тАФ assert `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS['idea'] == 'IDEA'` and that the translation function returns `'IDEA'` for a local `idea` ticket.
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_inbound_differ.py` (or `test_inbound_translate.py`) тАФ assert `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS['IDEA'] == 'idea'` and inbound translation returns `idea`.
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py` (or a preflight test) тАФ assert the preflight check does not raise on a config containing `idea`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the What section and Scope the specific test files/cases that exercise the outbound translation function (e.g. `test_outbound_fields.py`) and the inbound translation function (e.g. `test_inbound_differ.py` or `test_inbound_translate.py`) so that AC7 and AC8 have a described, scoped test home.
findings.finding: AC7 and AC8 require test-verified outbound and inbound translation function behavior, but the What section describes only 'Extend the parity test to assert the ideaтЖФIDEA entries' and the Scope lists only `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py` as a test file. No translation-function tests (exercising the actual translate/differ call paths) are described or scoped, leaving AC7 and AC8 as uncovered criteria.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria тАФ AC7, AC8; Scope тАФ tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py; What тАФ 'Extend the parity test'
findings.suggested_fix: In What, add: 'Add a test case in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_outbound_fields.py asserting that translating a local `idea` status produces Jira `IDEA`, and a test case in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_inbound_differ.py (or equivalent) asserting that a Jira `IDEA` inbound resolves to local `idea`.' Add these two test files to Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a parity assertion to the test suite (e.g. in `test_config.py`) verifying that `config.local_to_jira_status` and `outbound_fields._LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS` are identical, mirroring the existing `config.jira_to_local_status` тЖФ `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` parity test.
findings.finding: The plan adds 'idea тЖТ IDEA' to config.local_to_jira_status and outbound_fields._LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS independently, but there is no parity test or enforcement mechanism between these two maps. The existing parity enforcement covers only config.jira_to_local_status тЖФ inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS. If config.local_to_jira_status and outbound_fields._LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS drift (e.g. 'idea' added to one but not the other), the preflight uses config but the field-diff uses outbound_fields, creating a silent mistranslation for the 'idea' case.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section, fifth bullet; AC items 1 and 5; Scope section listing both config.py and outbound_fields.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) name the existing test that exercises the preflight path and confirm it will cover `idea` after the map entries are added, or (b) add a brief note in What describing how AC9 is verified (e.g., 'the parity test implicitly covers preflight by asserting all maps agree, which is what preflight checks').
findings.finding: AC9 ('The status-mapping preflight does not abort on a local idea status') is stated as a testable acceptance criterion but neither the What section nor the Scope describes any test or verification mechanism for it тАФ leaving it an orphan criterion with no described test home.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria тАФ AC9; What section; Scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'Verify/update' with a definitive directive in the `inbound_translate.py` What bullet тАФ either 'Add `idea` to `_LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES`' (if the symbol must be updated) or 'Confirm `idea` already appears in `_LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES`; no change needed' (if it is already present), so the executor has a clear action without investigation.
findings.finding: The What bullet 'Verify/update the local-status vocabulary reference in `inbound_translate.py`' uses investigation-deferring language ('Verify/update') without deciding which branch applies, making it a placeholder that an agent must resolve mid-execution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.md` to the Scope file list and commit to updating it with a cross-reference to the new reconciler/Jira setup doc, rather than leaving the link aspirational.
findings.finding: CLAUDE.md is described as something that 'can cross-reference' the new setup doc but is NOT listed in the Scope section as a file to be changed. This makes the cross-reference aspirational rather than committed, reducing discoverability of the new doc and the deployment-sequencing constraint.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) and 'What' bullet: 'a reconciler/Jira setup doc that CLAUDE.md can cross-reference'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to the Scope section: `CLAUDE.md тАФ add a cross-reference to the new reconciler/Jira setup doc under the reconciler configuration section.` Also add a corresponding AC: `CLAUDE.md links to the reconciler/Jira setup doc.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's description of `inbound_translate._LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES`: clarify that it is documentation-only and does NOT gate the `_jira_status_to_local` fallback path; the 'no fallback to open' guarantee (AC item 10) comes solely from adding `IDEA тЖТ idea` to `config.jira_to_local_status`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that updating `inbound_translate._LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES` will ensure 'no fallback to open for a Jira IDEA inbound' (AC item 10), but reading the actual code shows _LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES is documented as 'for documentation / debug purposes only тАФ the typed-mutation inbound path no longer uses value-membership.' The actual guard against the 'open' fallback is _jira_status_to_local, which reads config.jira_to_local_status at runtime. The plan's AC framing implies _LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES is the gating check, which is incorrect.
findings.location: Plan 'What' bullet: 'Verify/update the local-status vocabulary reference in inbound_translate.py (_LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES and the _jira_status_to_local default path)'; AC item 10

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the What section (or AC list) that the pre-existing `test_jira_to_local_status_round_trips_through_forward_map` test implicitly requires both `idea тЖТ IDEA` (forward) and `IDEA тЖТ idea` (reverse) map entries to be added atomically, to avoid a transient test failure during implementation.
findings.finding: The existing test `test_jira_to_local_status_round_trips_through_forward_map` in `test_config.py` (lines 131тАУ145) asserts that every value in `jira_to_local_status` is a key in `local_to_jira_status`. Adding `"IDEA" тЖТ idea` to `jira_to_local_status` without a corresponding `idea тЖТ "IDEA"` entry in `local_to_jira_status` would cause this test to fail. The plan does add both entries, so the test passes тАФ BUT the plan's AC list omits this pre-existing test as a passing constraint, and an implementer who adds the inbound map entry before the forward entry (or forgets the forward entry) will see a confusing failure from this unmentioned test.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria тАФ no mention of test_jira_to_local_status_round_trips_through_forward_map

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add) that the preflight StatusMappingError and/or the transition RuntimeError include a log message that names the specific missing status ('IDEA') and references the operator-prerequisite doc, so the failure is actionable without source-code archaeology.
findings.finding: The plan calls out a LOAD-BEARING deployment-sequencing risk тАФ shipping before IDEA is provisioned in Jira aborts reconciler passes тАФ but does not specify whether the resulting preflight StatusMappingError or RuntimeError from transition_issue_by_name will be instrumented (logged with enough context, emitted as a metric, or surfaced in an alert) so operators can distinguish 'ideaтЖТIDEA translation failed because IDEA is missing from Jira workflow' from other reconciler failures.
findings.location: Operator prerequisite + deployment sequencing section; AC line: 'The status-mapping preflight does not abort on a local idea status.'
findings.suggested_fix: In the preflight check or the exception handler for transition_issue_by_name, emit a structured log entry at ERROR level, e.g.: `log.error('Jira status not found in workflow: %s тАФ see docs/reconciler-jira-setup.md for provisioning steps', jira_status)`. Reference the new operator-prerequisite doc URL in the message.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
